enrichment.short_description: Cloud-native artifact repository manager that stores, secures, and distributes binaries, containers, and AI/ML models across 20+ package formats.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sonatype Nexus Repository manages Maven, npm, Docker, PyPI, and 20+ other package formats, with a free Community edition and paid Pro tier.

features.title: 20+ Package Formats
features.description: Stores and manages artifacts across Maven, npm, Docker, PyPI, Helm, Hugging Face, and other formats from a single repository manager.

features.title: Proxy Repository Caching
features.description: Caches remote dependencies locally to reduce build latency, with the vendor citing up to 95% faster builds.

features.title: RBAC, SAML, and SSO
features.description: Supports role-based access control, TLS encryption, and SAML/SSO integration for enterprise authentication.

features.title: Repository Firewall Integration
features.description: Integrates with Sonatype Repository Firewall to surface malware detection alerts on incoming components.

features.title: High Availability Clustering
features.description: Supports HA cluster deployments with a 99.9% uptime SLA for production environments.

features.title: Air-Gapped Deployment
features.description: Offers a disconnected deployment option for restricted or air-gapped environments.

pros: Supports 20+ package formats in a single repository manager
Free Community edition available for smaller teams
Enterprise security features like SAML/SSO and RBAC
Deployable self-hosted, in the cloud, or air-gapped
Integrates with Repository Firewall for malware detection
cons: Specific Pro edition pricing is not published; requires contacting sales
Advanced authentication (SSO/SAML) requires the paid Pro edition
Enterprise-focused feature set may be more than small teams need

faqs.question: Is Sonatype Nexus Repository free?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Community edition is available at no cost; a paid Pro edition adds features like SSO/SAML authentication.

faqs.question: Can Nexus Repository be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can be deployed self-hosted in a data center or cloud, in addition to a fully managed SaaS option.

faqs.question: What package formats does Nexus Repository support?
faqs.answer: It supports 20+ formats including Maven, npm, Docker, PyPI, Helm, and Hugging Face models.

faqs.question: Does Nexus Repository support air-gapped environments?
faqs.answer: Yes, a disconnected/air-gapped deployment option is available for restricted environments.

use_cases.title: Centralizing build artifacts
use_cases.description: Teams use Nexus Repository to store and version build outputs like Maven and npm packages in one place.

use_cases.title: Securing the software supply chain
use_cases.description: Organizations use RBAC, SAML/SSO, and Repository Firewall integration to control access and screen incoming components.

use_cases.title: Reducing build times with caching
use_cases.description: Proxy repositories cache remote dependencies locally to speed up CI/CD pipelines.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sonatype Nexus Repository?
paragraphs: Sonatype Nexus Repository is an artifact repository manager that stores, manages, and distributes software binaries, containers, and AI/ML models. It supports more than 20 package formats, including Maven, npm, Docker, PyPI, Helm, and Hugging Face model artifacts, from a single centralized repository.

The product is offered as a free Community edition and a paid Pro edition with enterprise authentication (SAML/SSO), high availability clustering, and integration with Sonatype's Repository Firewall for malware detection. It can be deployed self-hosted, as a managed cloud service, or in an air-gapped configuration for restricted environments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sonatype Nexus Repository is for
paragraphs: Nexus Repository is built for development, DevOps, and security teams that need to centralize build artifacts, cache dependencies for faster CI/CD pipelines, and enforce access controls and supply-chain security across multiple package ecosystems.

enrichment.short_description: Universal artifact repository manager supporting 50+ package formats for managing binaries, containers, and AI/ML models across the software supply chain.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: JFrog Artifactory manages 50+ package formats including Maven, npm, Docker, and Helm, with free, SaaS, and self-managed enterprise options.

features.title: 50+ Package Formats
features.description: Manages Maven, npm, PyPI, Docker, Helm, NuGet, Debian, RPM, Terraform, and AI/ML model artifacts in one platform.

features.title: Container & Kubernetes Registry
features.description: Provides a Docker-compatible container registry with Kubernetes-focused registry capabilities.

features.title: Dependency Proxying & Caching
features.description: Proxies and caches remote dependencies to reduce external network calls during builds.

features.title: Bi-Directional Replication
features.description: Automates repository synchronization across multiple sites for distributed teams.

features.title: Software Governance
features.description: Applies RBAC and security policy enforcement across the artifact lifecycle.

features.title: High Availability SLA
features.description: Offers a 99.99% uptime SLA on premium availability tiers.

pros: Broadest package format support among major artifact repository managers (50+)
Deployable as SaaS, self-managed, or hybrid
Used by 6,500+ teams including 80% of the Fortune 100
100+ ecosystem integrations, including GitHub, ServiceNow, and Sonar
cons: Specific pricing tiers are not published on the marketing site; requires contacting sales
Feature breadth can add setup complexity for small teams
Premium SLA and advanced features are reserved for paid tiers

faqs.question: Is there a free tier of JFrog Artifactory?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free tier is available to try, alongside paid Enterprise+ options.

faqs.question: Can Artifactory run on-premises?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports self-managed on-premises or multi-cloud deployment in addition to SaaS.

faqs.question: What package formats does Artifactory support?
faqs.answer: It supports 50+ formats, including Maven, npm, Docker, Helm, NuGet, and Terraform.

faqs.question: Does Artifactory offer high availability?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Premium Availability SLA option offers 99.99% uptime.

use_cases.title: Managing containers at scale
use_cases.description: Teams use Artifactory's Docker-compatible registry to store and distribute container images.

use_cases.title: Distributed team synchronization
use_cases.description: Bi-directional replication keeps repositories in sync across global development sites.

use_cases.title: Enterprise governance
use_cases.description: RBAC and policy enforcement help large organizations control access to artifacts.

features: Try Artifactory with core repository features
Advanced governance and security policy enforcement

long_form_blocks.heading: What is JFrog Artifactory?
paragraphs: JFrog Artifactory is a universal artifact repository manager that centralizes storage and distribution of software binaries, packages, containers, and releases. It supports more than 50 package formats, including Maven, npm, PyPI, Docker, Helm, NuGet, Debian, RPM, and Terraform, as well as AI/ML model artifacts.

Artifactory can be deployed as a fully managed SaaS on AWS, Azure, or GCP, self-managed on-premises or across multiple clouds, or in a hybrid configuration. It is used by more than 6,500 teams, including 80% of the Fortune 100, and integrates with over 100 tools in the software delivery ecosystem.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who JFrog Artifactory is for
paragraphs: Artifactory is built for enterprise development and platform teams that need a single repository manager across many package formats, distributed replication for global teams, and governance controls for large-scale software supply chains.

enrichment.short_description: Open source platform for fetching, hosting, and distributing software repositories across RPM, Debian, Python, npm, Container, and other formats.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pulp is a free, open source (GPL-2.0) repository manager for mirroring and distributing RPM, Debian, Python, npm, and Container packages.

features.title: Multi-Format Repository Support
features.description: Manages RPM, Debian, Python, npm, Container, Maven, and Ansible content, among other formats.

features.title: On-Demand & Synced Repositories
features.description: Supports on-demand downloading as well as fully synchronized local mirrors of upstream repositories.

features.title: Role-Based Access Control
features.description: Includes RBAC and domain support for multi-tenant repository management.

features.title: Lifecycle Promotion & Replication
features.description: Supports promoting content through lifecycle stages and replicating repositories across instances.

features.title: Metadata Signing & Vulnerability Scanning
features.description: Provides metadata signing and vulnerability scanning capabilities for hosted content.

features.title: Kubernetes Deployment via Pulp Operator
features.description: Deploys through OCI container images or Kubernetes using the Pulp Operator.

pros: Fully open source under GPL-2.0-or-later with no paid tier required
Broad package format support (RPM, Debian, Python, npm, Container, Maven, Ansible)
Actively maintained, with releases as recent as July 2026
REST API for programmatic repository management
Deployable via Kubernetes Operator or OCI container images
cons: No official managed/hosted SaaS offering; requires self-hosting
No commercial support contract mentioned on the official site
Kubernetes/OCI-based deployment adds operational complexity for small teams

faqs.question: Is Pulp free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pulp is fully open source with no commercial pricing tier.

faqs.question: What package formats does Pulp support?
faqs.answer: It supports RPM, Debian, Python, npm, Container, Maven, Ansible, and more.

faqs.question: Can Pulp be deployed on Kubernetes?
faqs.answer: Yes, via the Pulp Operator, in addition to OCI container image deployment.

faqs.question: What license does Pulp use?
faqs.answer: Pulp's pulpcore codebase is licensed under GPL-2.0-or-later.

use_cases.title: Mirroring upstream repositories
use_cases.description: Organizations use Pulp to mirror RPM, Debian, and Python repositories locally to reduce external dependency.

use_cases.title: Air-gapped package distribution
use_cases.description: Pulp hosts custom and mirrored content for environments without direct internet access.

use_cases.title: Multi-format artifact management
use_cases.description: Teams consolidate content from multiple package ecosystems into one platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pulp?
paragraphs: Pulp is an open source platform that lets developers fetch, upload, organize, and distribute software packages on-premises or in the cloud. It supports repository content types including RPM, Debian, Python, npm, Container, Maven, and Ansible.

The project, maintained on GitHub as pulp/pulpcore under a GPL-2.0-or-later license, supports on-demand downloading, synchronized mirroring, RBAC, lifecycle promotion, metadata signing, and vulnerability scanning. It can be deployed via OCI container images or Kubernetes using the Pulp Operator.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pulp is for
paragraphs: Pulp is aimed at developers and system administrators who need to mirror, host, and distribute software packages across multiple ecosystems, including teams operating in air-gapped or restricted-network environments.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-native artifact management platform for securely distributing software packages and containers across 30+ formats with usage-based pricing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cloudsmith is a managed artifact and container registry supporting 30+ package formats, with a free Core tier and usage-based paid plans.

features.title: 30+ Package Formats
features.description: Supports 30+ package formats plus raw file hosting in a single managed platform.

features.title: OCI-Compliant Container Registry
features.description: Provides a Docker-compatible, OCI-compliant container registry.

features.title: Security & Policy Controls
features.description: Includes vulnerability identification, malware detection, and policy management using OPA Rego syntax.

features.title: Package Workflows
features.description: Supports quarantine, promotion, and package signing workflows.

features.title: Enterprise Access Controls
features.description: Offers SAML/SSO, SCIM provisioning, and OIDC token support.

features.title: Global Distribution Network
features.description: Uses a distribution network the vendor describes as 600 global points of presence with edge caching.

pros: Free Core tier available for open-source repositories
Fully managed SaaS, no infrastructure to maintain
Supports 30+ package formats plus raw files
Usage-based pricing means uploads are free and only delivery/storage are billed
cons: Free Core tier is capped at 500MB storage and 1GB delivery with no overage option
Paid plan pricing beyond Core is usage-based and not fully published
Enterprise features like SSO/SCIM require paid plans

faqs.question: Does Cloudsmith have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Core plan is free but limited to 500MB storage and 1GB delivery per month.

faqs.question: How is Cloudsmith priced?
faqs.answer: It uses usage-based pricing on storage and package delivery/bandwidth; uploads are free of charge.

faqs.question: What package formats does Cloudsmith support?
faqs.answer: It supports 30+ formats plus raw files, including an OCI-compliant container registry.

faqs.question: Is Cloudsmith self-hosted or SaaS?
faqs.answer: Cloudsmith is a fully managed, cloud-native SaaS platform.

use_cases.title: Hosting open-source packages
use_cases.description: Open-source projects use the free Core tier to distribute packages publicly.

use_cases.title: Securing the container supply chain
use_cases.description: Teams use the OCI-compliant registry with vulnerability and malware scanning to secure container images.

use_cases.title: Enterprise package governance
use_cases.description: Organizations use SAML/SSO, SCIM, and OPA-based policy management to control access to artifacts.

features: No overage — upgrade required beyond limits

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cloudsmith?
paragraphs: Cloudsmith is a fully managed, cloud-native artifact management platform for controlling, securing, and distributing software packages and containers. It supports more than 30 package formats plus raw files, along with a Docker-compatible, OCI-compliant container registry.

The platform is priced on usage, primarily storage and package delivery/bandwidth, with uploads free of charge. A free Core plan is capped at 500MB storage and 1GB delivery per month, while paid Pro, Ultra, and Enterprise plans add higher allowances and enterprise access controls such as SAML/SSO and SCIM provisioning.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Cloudsmith is for
paragraphs: Cloudsmith is aimed at open-source maintainers who want free public package hosting, and at enterprise teams that need a managed, secure, multi-format artifact and container registry without operating their own infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Open source IT infrastructure monitoring engine for servers, networks, and applications, with a paid Nagios XI edition for enterprise dashboards.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Nagios Core is free, open source IT monitoring software for servers and networks, backed by a paid Nagios XI enterprise edition.

features.title: Plugin Architecture
features.description: Uses a plugin-based architecture with thousands of available community plugins, including check_ping, check_ssh, check_cpu, and check_memory.

features.title: Cross-Platform Agent (NCPA)
features.description: Provides a cross-platform agent for real-time monitoring of Windows, Linux, and network systems.

features.title: Scalability Tools
features.description: Includes Mod-Gearman for distributing checks across large environments.

features.title: Configuration & Visualization Add-ons
features.description: Offers configuration tools and dashboard/network map add-ons.

features.title: Nagios XI Dashboards
features.description: The paid Nagios XI edition adds pre-installed plugins, monitoring wizards, and enterprise dashboards.

pros: Nagios Core is free and open source
Large plugin ecosystem with thousands of community-built checks
25+ years of development history and enterprise adoption
Scalable to large environments via Mod-Gearman
cons: Nagios XI pricing is not published; requires contacting sales
Core's interface and configuration are less modern than newer monitoring tools
Advanced dashboards and wizards require the paid XI edition

faqs.question: Is Nagios free?
faqs.answer: Nagios Core, the monitoring engine, is free and open source. Nagios XI is a paid enterprise edition.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Nagios Core and Nagios XI?
faqs.answer: Nagios XI adds pre-installed plugins, monitoring wizards, and dashboards on top of the free Core engine.

faqs.question: Does Nagios support Windows and Linux?
faqs.answer: Yes, via the cross-platform NCPA agent.

faqs.question: Can Nagios scale to large environments?
faqs.answer: Yes, using tools like Mod-Gearman to distribute checks.

use_cases.title: Monitoring on-prem infrastructure
use_cases.description: Teams use Nagios Core to monitor servers, network devices, and applications for downtime.

use_cases.title: Enterprise monitoring with dashboards
use_cases.description: Organizations upgrade to Nagios XI for pre-built wizards and visual dashboards.

use_cases.title: Custom monitoring checks
use_cases.description: Teams write or install plugins to monitor virtually any service or metric.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Nagios?
paragraphs: Nagios is an IT infrastructure monitoring platform described by its maker as "the industry standard in open source IT monitoring tools." Nagios Core, the free and open source monitoring engine, watches servers, networks, applications, and services to help prevent downtime.

Nagios uses a plugin architecture with thousands of available community-built checks, plus a cross-platform agent (NCPA) and scalability tooling like Mod-Gearman. A paid enterprise edition, Nagios XI, adds pre-installed plugins, monitoring wizards, and dashboards on top of the free Core engine.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Nagios is for
paragraphs: Nagios suits system administrators and IT operations teams that want a free, highly extensible monitoring engine, as well as enterprises that want pre-built dashboards and wizards through the paid Nagios XI edition.

enrichment.short_description: Unified IT monitoring platform for hybrid infrastructure with a free Community edition and paid Pro, Ultimate, and Cloud tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Checkmk is an IT observability platform with 2,000+ integrations, a free open source Community edition, and paid Pro/Ultimate/Cloud plans.

features.title: Full-Stack Monitoring
features.description: Monitors servers, networks, storage, cloud workloads, containers, and applications from one interface.

features.title: 2,000+ Integrations
features.description: Offers over 2,000 integrations for infrastructure and application monitoring.

features.title: AI-Powered Root Cause Analysis
features.description: Uses AI-assisted analysis to help identify the root cause of incidents.

features.title: Automated Discovery
features.description: Automatically discovers hosts and services on the network.

features.title: Dependency Mapping
features.description: Maps business services to the underlying infrastructure components they depend on.

features.title: Multi-Tenancy
features.description: Supports data segregation for managed service providers with multiple customers.

pros: Free, open source Community edition supports up to roughly 100 hosts
2,000+ built-in integrations reduce custom setup work
Available both self-hosted and as managed SaaS (CloudAI)
Used by large enterprises including Adobe, Siemens, and Volkswagen
cons: Community edition is capped at roughly 100 hosts
Paid tiers start at €190/month and scale by host count
AI root cause analysis and advanced automation require paid tiers

faqs.question: Is Checkmk free?
faqs.answer: The Community edition is free and open source, limited to roughly 100 hosts.

faqs.question: How much does Checkmk Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Pro starts at €190/month billed annually, roughly €1.90 per host.

faqs.question: Does Checkmk offer a cloud/SaaS option?
faqs.answer: Yes, CloudAI is a fully managed SaaS edition starting at €240/month per year for around 1,000 services.

faqs.question: What does Checkmk monitor?
faqs.answer: Servers, networks, storage, cloud workloads, containers, and applications.

use_cases.title: Small infrastructure monitoring
use_cases.description: Teams with up to roughly 100 hosts use the free Community edition.

use_cases.title: Hybrid cloud observability
use_cases.description: Enterprises use Pro/Ultimate editions to monitor on-prem and cloud resources together.

use_cases.title: Managed service provider monitoring
use_cases.description: MSPs use multi-tenancy features to monitor multiple customer environments from one instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Checkmk?
paragraphs: Checkmk is a unified IT observability platform for monitoring hybrid and cloud environments, covering servers, networks, storage, cloud workloads, containers, and applications from a single interface. It offers over 2,000 integrations plus automated host/service discovery and dependency mapping.

Checkmk is available as a free, open source Community edition capped at around 100 hosts, alongside paid self-hosted Pro and Ultimate editions and a fully managed CloudAI SaaS edition. Pro starts at €190/month billed annually, and Ultimate starts at €275/month billed annually.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Checkmk is for
paragraphs: Checkmk fits small IT teams monitoring a limited number of hosts on the free Community edition, as well as larger enterprises and managed service providers that need full-stack observability, multi-tenancy, and SLA-backed support.

enrichment.short_description: Open source, auto-discovering network monitoring system supporting SNMP, CDP, LLDP, OSPF, and BGP across a wide range of network hardware.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LibreNMS is a free, open source (GPLv3) network monitoring system with auto-discovery, a REST API, and native iOS/Android apps.

features.title: Automatic Network Discovery
features.description: Discovers devices via CDP, FDP, LLDP, OSPF, BGP, SNMP, and ARP.

features.title: Flexible Alerting
features.description: Sends alerts via email, IRC, Slack, and other channels.

features.title: Full REST API
features.description: Provides a full API for retrieving and managing monitoring data.

features.title: Bandwidth Billing
features.description: Supports bandwidth billing based on port usage.

features.title: Distributed Polling
features.description: Scales monitoring across multiple pollers with automatic updates.

features.title: Native Mobile Apps
features.description: Offers native iOS and Android apps for monitoring on the go.

pros: Fully open source under GPLv3 with no paid tier
Actively maintained, with 173 releases and a June 2026 release
Broad device support (Cisco, Linux, FreeBSD, Juniper, Brocade, HP, and more)
Native mobile apps for iOS and Android
Large contributor community (1,300+ contributors)
cons: No official managed/hosted SaaS offering; requires self-hosting
PHP/MySQL stack requires manual server setup and maintenance
No official vendor support contract mentioned

faqs.question: Is LibreNMS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, LibreNMS is fully open source under GPLv3 with no paid tier.

faqs.question: What protocols does LibreNMS use for discovery?
faqs.answer: CDP, FDP, LLDP, OSPF, BGP, SNMP, and ARP.

faqs.question: Does LibreNMS have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, native apps are available for iOS and Android.

faqs.question: How is LibreNMS deployed?
faqs.answer: Self-hosted on CentOS/Ubuntu or via Docker.

use_cases.title: Multi-vendor network monitoring
use_cases.description: Network admins monitor mixed-vendor environments including Cisco, Juniper, and HP hardware.

use_cases.title: ISP bandwidth billing
use_cases.description: Service providers use port-based bandwidth billing for customer usage tracking.

use_cases.title: Distributed enterprise monitoring
use_cases.description: Large organizations use distributed polling to scale monitoring across many sites.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LibreNMS?
paragraphs: LibreNMS is a fully featured, open source network monitoring system with automatic device discovery via CDP, FDP, LLDP, OSPF, BGP, SNMP, and ARP. It is built on a PHP/MySQL/SNMP stack and supports a wide range of network hardware and operating systems, including Cisco, Linux, FreeBSD, Juniper, Brocade, and HP.

The project is licensed under GPLv3, hosted on GitHub, and actively maintained, with 173 releases and over 1,300 contributors as of its most recent release. It offers a full REST API, flexible alerting (email, IRC, Slack), bandwidth billing, distributed polling, and native iOS and Android apps.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LibreNMS is for
paragraphs: LibreNMS is built for network administrators and service providers who manage multi-vendor network infrastructure and want a free, self-hosted monitoring system with broad device support and an API for automation.

enrichment.short_description: Network monitoring platform with a free Community edition and paid Professional and Enterprise tiers for SMEs, ISPs, and enterprises.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Observium monitors network devices with auto-discovery and a RESTful API, offering a free Community edition and paid Professional/Enterprise tiers.

features.title: Automatic Discovery
features.description: Automatically discovers network devices and services, supporting 458+ OS types and vendors.

features.title: Real-Time & Historical Metrics
features.description: Collects and displays real-time and historical performance metrics via a web interface.

features.title: Rule-Based Grouping
features.description: Automatically groups devices and generates network maps using rules.

features.title: Alerting
features.description: Supports threshold, state, and syslog-based alerting.

features.title: Traffic Accounting & Billing
features.description: Includes a traffic accounting and billing system.

features.title: RESTful API
features.description: Provides a RESTful API for external integration.

pros: Free Community edition available for home labs and small deployments
Supports 458+ OS types and device vendors
Professional/Enterprise tiers get faster, more frequent SVN-based updates
Enterprise plan includes 10 hours of support per year
cons: Community edition only receives updates every 6-12 months
Professional (£250/year) and Enterprise (£1,500/year) plans add ongoing cost
Distributed under the QPL license, which is less common than typical open-source licenses

faqs.question: Is Observium free?
faqs.answer: The Community edition is free under the QPL license; Professional and Enterprise are paid annual plans.

faqs.question: How much does Observium Professional cost?
faqs.answer: £250 per year.

faqs.question: How much does Observium Enterprise cost?
faqs.answer: £1,500 per year, including 10 hours of support.

faqs.question: How often is Observium updated?
faqs.answer: Community edition every 6-12 months; Professional/Enterprise receive daily SVN-based releases.

use_cases.title: Home lab monitoring
use_cases.description: Hobbyists use the free Community edition to monitor home network devices.

use_cases.title: ISP network monitoring
use_cases.description: Internet service providers use the Professional plan for device monitoring and traffic billing.

use_cases.title: Large enterprise network monitoring
use_cases.description: Enterprises use the Enterprise plan for broad device support and support hours.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Observium?
paragraphs: Observium is a self-hosted network monitoring platform that provides real-time and historical visibility into network health and performance through automatic device discovery, metric collection, and alerting. It supports over 458 OS types and device vendors, including Cisco, Windows, Linux, HP, Juniper, Dell, and NetApp.

Observium's Community edition is free and distributed under the QPL open source license, receiving updates every 6-12 months. Paid Professional (£250/year) and Enterprise (£1,500/year, including 10 hours of support) editions receive daily SVN-based releases and are aimed at SMEs, ISPs, and larger enterprises.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Observium is for
paragraphs: Observium suits home lab users and small deployments on the free Community edition, and SMEs, ISPs, and enterprises that need faster update cycles and support through the paid Professional and Enterprise plans.

enrichment.short_description: Open source, developer-oriented health dashboard for HTTP, ICMP, TCP, and DNS checks, with an optional paid hosted version at Gatus.io.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gatus is a free, open source (Apache 2.0) health dashboard and status page tool with 30+ alerting integrations and an optional hosted plan.

features.title: Multi-Protocol Health Checks
features.description: Monitors services via HTTP, ICMP, TCP, and DNS queries.

features.title: Configurable Conditions
features.description: Evaluates checks using conditions on status codes, response time, body content, and IP validation.

features.title: Extensive Alerting
features.description: Integrates with 30+ providers including Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, Discord, and Opsgenie, plus custom webhooks.

features.title: Metrics Exposure
features.description: Exposes metrics for external monitoring systems.

features.title: Badge Generation
features.description: Generates uptime and performance badges.

features.title: Low Resource Usage
features.description: Designed for minimal resource consumption when self-hosted.

pros: Free and open source under Apache 2.0, with 11.5k GitHub stars
Supports 30+ alerting integrations out of the box
Deployable via Docker, Kubernetes (Helm), or a standalone binary
Optional managed/hosted version available for teams that don't want to self-host
cons: Hosted Gatus.io plan pricing is not fully published
Self-hosted deployment requires operational setup and maintenance
Primarily configuration-file driven, which may require YAML familiarity

faqs.question: Is Gatus free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted version is free and open source under Apache 2.0. A paid hosted version is also available at Gatus.io.

faqs.question: How is Gatus deployed?
faqs.answer: Via Docker, Kubernetes with Helm, or as a standalone binary when self-hosting.

faqs.question: What alerting integrations does Gatus support?
faqs.answer: 30+ providers including Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, Discord, and custom webhooks.

faqs.question: Can Gatus be used for status pages?
faqs.answer: Yes, it functions as a developer-oriented status page and health dashboard.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted service monitoring
use_cases.description: Teams self-host Gatus to monitor HTTP, TCP, ICMP, and DNS-based services.

use_cases.title: Public status pages
use_cases.description: Teams use Gatus to communicate service uptime and incidents to users.

use_cases.title: Managed monitoring without self-hosting
use_cases.description: Teams that prefer not to self-host use the Gatus.io hosted plan.
use_cases.audience: Smaller teams without dedicated ops resources

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gatus?
paragraphs: Gatus is a developer-oriented health dashboard that monitors services through HTTP, ICMP, TCP, and DNS queries, evaluating results against configurable conditions such as status codes, response time, and body content. It is open source under the Apache 2.0 license and has over 11,000 GitHub stars.

It can be self-hosted via Docker, Kubernetes (Helm), or a standalone binary, and supports 30+ alerting integrations including Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, and Discord. For teams that prefer not to self-host, a managed hosted version is available at Gatus.io.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gatus is for
paragraphs: Gatus is aimed at developers and operations teams who want a lightweight, self-hostable health dashboard and status page, as well as smaller teams that prefer the managed Gatus.io hosted option instead of running their own infrastructure.

name: Cabot
skip_reason: Project is explicitly unmaintained: the official README states 'Cabot is stable...but it is not actively maintained' and the maintainers are seeking new owners. The latest release (0.11.7) shipped in September 2017 with no meaningful activity since.
